operating_state: RTX delivered total net sales of $88.6 billion in 2025, representing $7.9 billion growth driven by organic expansion across all segments partially offset by net divestitures. Commercial aerospace comprised 48% of revenue while defense represented 53%, with organic growth led by Pratt & Whitney aftermarket recovery, Collins aftermarket expansion, and Raytheon Patriot programs. Operating profit margin expanded to 10.5% from 8.1% in 2024 despite negative EAC adjustments and ongoing PW1100 GTF powder metal inspection costs.
cost_structure: Total cost of sales increased to $70.8 billion representing 80% of net sales compared to 81% in 2024, reflecting improved gross margin performance despite inflationary pressures in materials, components, and labor rates. Operating profit increased organically driven by higher aftermarket volumes offset by tariff impacts, accelerated PW1100 fleet inspections, compliance expenditures under deferred prosecution agreements, and Collins impairment charges from contract cancellations and supply chain disruptions.
material_events: RTX entered deferred prosecution agreements and SEC administrative order in October 2024 resolving FCPA violations and defective pricing claims, followed by a three-year State Department Consent Agreement requiring external compliance audits. The company completed divestitures, repaid long-term debt, initiated a pension buy-out transferring obligations for 60,000 retirees to Prudential, and completed a $10 billion accelerated share repurchase program in September 2024.
macro_exposures: Global supply chain disruptions continue affecting raw materials procurement including rare earth elements and microelectronics, with U.S. government tariffs imposed on all trading partners since February 2025 creating headwinds for commercial operations. Chinese sanctions target Raytheon and Collins joint venture related to Taiwan arms sales, while Middle East customer contract disruptions affected operations though certain contracts restarted in Q4 2024. The January 2026 Executive Order grants the Secretary of War potential authority to limit dividend payments and share repurchases based on government contract performance assessments.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery with aftermarket growth momentum supported by defense bookings and Raytheon's backlog providing multi-year revenue visibility. Congress enacted supplementary DoW funding through 2029 including funding for the Golden Dome missile defense project where RTX is well-positioned. PW1100 GTF powder metal inspections will continue driving elevated aircraft-on-ground levels through 2026 with estimated cash impact, while the pension buy-out transaction will generate a non-cash settlement charge in Q4 2025.
uncertainty_profile: Three-year deferred prosecution agreement compliance creates potential for criminal prosecution and government contracting suspension if terms are violated, with ongoing contract audits presenting risks of significant payment recoveries. PW1100 powder metal remediation costs depend on inspection results, shop visit timing, parts availability, and potential expansion to additional engine models with customer compensation accruals declining. Purchase obligations represent government contracts with termination protection and commercial aerospace financing commitments requiring market condition management.
